linkage_explanation: The hypothesis directly references the expert-novice performance difference, a core prediction of the Information-Processing/Expert-Novice Paradigm.

hypothesis_text: The amplitudes of both cue-locked and target-locked ERP components would be influenced by prior cues.
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis that neural markers (ERP amplitudes) will be modulated by prior cues is an abductive inference from the general Bayesian principle to a specific neural correlate.

hypothesis_text: The ERP components would show a between-group effect (experts vs. novices).
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis extends the expert-novice behavioral difference paradigm to the neural level, which is a common and consistent application of the framework.

hypothesis_text: Those who used the spatial occlusion goggles during the training intervention would benefit through (i) a significant improvement in pass accuracy, reducing outcome error
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis is consistent with the information-processing view that training with degraded visual information (spatial occlusion) forces adaptation and improves skill execution when full vision is restored.

hypothesis_text: Those who used the spatial occlusion goggles during the training intervention would benefit through (ii) a significant improvement in football control, reducing control error
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis aligns with the information-processing principle that limiting one information source (vision of limbs/ball) may enhance the use of other proprioceptive/kinaesthetic cues, improving motor control.

hypothesis_text: Those who used the spatial occlusion goggles during the training intervention would benefit through (iii) a significant improvement in concurrent number call task, reducing number call error, once full visual conditions were returned.
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis is consistent with an information-processing view that training under occlusion may reduce the attentional demands of the primary motor task, freeing cognitive resources for a secondary task.

hypothesis_text: Any reduction experienced in number call error would not occur as a result of an increase in control or outcome error.
linkage_explanation: This prediction is consistent with the framework's concept of efficient resource allocation and skill automation, where improved performance on one task does not necessitate a trade-off with another.

hypothesis_text: A sustained isometric knee contraction would induce both pain unpleasantness and pain intensity.
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis that exercise induces both sensory and affective pain dimensions is compatible with Multidimensional Pain Theory, which posits these distinct components.

hypothesis_text: Active tDCS with the anodal electrode placed over the left DLPFC would reduce ratings of exercise-induced pain unpleasantness, but not pain intensity, and enhance muscular endurance time when compared to a placebo control.
linked_theory: DLPFC Role In Pain Affect Regulation (Lorenz Et Al., 2003; Boggio Et Al., 2008)
mechanism_description: Anodal tDCS of the left DLPFC alleviates affective responses to pain (reduces pain unpleasantness) because the left DLPFC plays a major role in the motivational-affective aspect of pain, with its activation negatively correlated with pain unpleasantness.
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis is directly attributed to prior research on the DLPFC's role in pain affect and the analgesic (affect-reducing) effects of anodal tDCS applied to this region.

hypothesis_text: Sport-specific physical performance gains (repeated-sprint ability and on-field aerobic capacity) and vascular function adaptations will be larger after this innovative RSH protocol compared with RSN.
mechanism_description: Performing 'all-out' efforts in very severe hypoxia (~5000 m) may activate anaerobic and neuromuscular pathways beyond that which can be achieved at sea level or moderate hypoxia; RSH adaptive mechanisms include improved muscle blood perfusion, optimized oxygen extraction by fast-twitch fibers, and specific skeletal muscle adaptations through oxygen-sensing pathways (hypoxic-inducible factors).
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis is compatible with the general model that hypoxia induces specific physiological adaptations (e.g., vascular, muscular) that enhance performance, but it is not a strict logical deduction from that model.

hypothesis_text: An 8-week combined HIIT and dry-land MST program will improve maximum muscular strength, technique, and 100-m butterfly swimming performance in competitive age-group swimmers.
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis has no theoretical grounding; it is motivated by prior empirical findings and a gap in the literature.

hypothesis_text: The MF intervention would worsen players' game-based relevant performance responses such as physical enjoyment, total distance covered, and technical actions (e.g., lost balls and unsuccessful passes).
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis is consistent with the Psychobiological Model's core claim that mental fatigue negatively impacts performance, but it is not a strict logical deduction from the model.

hypothesis_text: The athlete group with poorer neurocognitive function would exhibit greater acceleration and rotational velocity following landing compared to the groups with higher neurocognitive function.
mechanism_description: The ability to rapidly make decisions in complex environments may influence the way individuals perform multifaceted sport-related tasks, leading to biomechanical patterns (like greater acceleration/rotational velocity) that may predispose them to injury.
linkage_explanation: The hypothesis is consistent with the model's core claim that poorer neurocognitive performance leads to deviations in lower extremity biomechanics, which are linked to injury risk.
